runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution elasticity for those in the 6th earnings decile within a U.S. tax-benefit microsimulation context.
runs.reasoningSummary: Primary earners generally exhibit low substitution elasticities compared to secondary earners. For the middle of the distribution (decile 6), the response is constrained by institutional factors and full-time work norms, though some flexibility exists regarding overtime or effort intensity.

runs.interpretation: Primary-earner substitution elasticity for earnings decile 6 in a U.S. tax-benefit microsimulation model.
runs.reasoningSummary: Empirical literature on intensive margin labor supply for primary earners consistently finds small positive elasticities, typically lower than those for secondary earners. Decile 6 represents middle-income earners where institutional constraints and fixed work hours often limit short-run responsiveness to marginal wage changes.

  "interpretation": "Primary-earner substitution elasticity for earnings decile 6 in a U.S. tax-benefit microsimulation model.",

  ],

}

runs.interpretation: The compensated substitution-response elasticity for primary earners in the 6th earnings decile in a U.S. tax-benefit microsimulation context.
runs.reasoningSummary: Primary earners generally exhibit lower labor supply elasticities than secondary earners. }
